title: physaliastrum stoloniferum
intro: Physaliastrum stoloniferum, also known as the "Devil's Claw" or "Harpagophytum procumbens," is a flowering plant native to southern Africa, characterized by its hook-like roots that allow it to attach to other plants and animals for support. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and pain-relieving effects. Some of the health benefits associated with Physaliastrum stoloniferum include relief from arthritis, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as improved digestion and immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of capsules, tablets, or tinctures, and can also be consumed as a tea or added to topical creams and ointments for localized relief.
